steps.title: Apply the model
steps.content: The energy ladder model explains fuel choice mainly through household income: as incomes rise, high-technology fuels replace solid fuels. An advocate of the model would therefore explain Brazil's decline in solid fuel use by pointing to rising incomes and greater use of high-technology fuels.

steps.title: Keep the model income based
steps.content: B uses fuel availability rather than income. C introduces relative fuel prices, which the model description does not emphasize. D says rising incomes weakened the link between income and fuel choice, contradicting the model. The explanation must follow the model's income-driven mechanism. The answer is A.
